input.background: Recall the tea-tasting challenge illustration based on whether a person could predict whether milk was added to a cup before or after tea is poured. The taster proved right in six of eight trials.
input.question: Based on this finding, determine the proportion that the tester can correctly identify in the long run.

output.answer: The $95 \%$ confidence interval by this method is 0.349 to 0.968.

correct_predictions.description: The number of correct predictions made by the taster in the trials.

total_trials.description: The total number of trials conducted in the tea-tasting challenge.

meta_info.caption: Confidence interval for proportion, exact method (Teachallenge).
meta_info.section: 16.5 CONFIDENCE INTERVAL FOR A POPULATION PROPORTION
